stem: Each row below names a CPU register and asks you to tick the role it performs.
model_answer: {"Program Counter (PC)": "Holds the address of the next instruction to fetch", "Memory Address Register (MAR)": "Holds the address of the memory cell being read or written", "Memory Data Register (MDR)": "Holds the data being transferred to or from memory", "Accumulator (ACC)": "Stores the result of the latest ALU calculation"}
review_notes: One tick per row. Common pupil confusion: PC vs MAR (both hold addresses).

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Tick the role that each register performs. One tick per row.

columns: Holds the address of the next instruction to fetch
Holds the address of the memory cell being read or written
Holds the data being transferred to or from memory
Stores the result of the latest ALU calculation
correctByRow: Holds the address of the next instruction to fetch
Holds the address of the memory cell being read or written
Holds the data being transferred to or from memory
Stores the result of the latest ALU calculation
